title: Bitbucket の 403 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article accurately identifies common causes of Bitbucket 403 errors, aligning with official documentation.
Key solutions, including permission management, IP whitelisting (allowlisting), and API token scopes, are correctly described.
The troubleshooting steps are comprehensive and practical, including details on clearing Git credentials and checking audit logs.

sources.title: Error 403 when performing Git operations | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: Bitbucket's 403 error indicates that an authenticated user does not have access permissions to the repository or resource. This error frequently occurs during Git push, pull, or repository settings changes. If an organization has enabled IP whitelisting, access from outside the allowed network range will be rejected with a 403 error.

sources.title: Bitbucket deployment fails with error 403 - Auth0 Community
sources.claim: Bitbucket's 403 error indicates that an authenticated user does not have access permissions to the repository or resource. In other words, the user exists and has successfully logged into Bitbucket, but lacks the necessary operational permissions for a specific repository.

sources.title: Bitbucket Integration: 403 Forbidden on Webhook Creation Despite Correct Permissions + Stale Data After Re-integration · Issue #624 · kodustech/kodus-ai - GitHub
sources.claim: Bitbucket's 403 error indicates that an authenticated user does not have access permissions to the repository or resource. A 403 error also occurs when a user with Write permission attempts to access administrative functions.

sources.title: 403 Error When Pulling from Repository After 6 Months of Normal Usage
sources.claim: This error frequently occurs during Git push, pull, or repository settings changes. Example Git error message: `fatal: unable to access 'https://bitbucket.org/<workspace>/<repository>.git/': The requested URL returned error: 403`

sources.title: How to solve the requested URL returned error: 403 in git repository - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: Example Git error message: `fatal: unable to access 'https://bitbucket.org/<workspace>/<repository>.git/': The requested URL returned error: 403`

sources.title: 4 Levels of Bitbucket Server Permissions | Bitbucket Data Center - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: If repository access level is limited to read-only, write operations like push or settings changes are rejected. With Read, only pull is possible; granting write permission enables push. Bitbucket has different role settings at the Workspace level and Repository level.

sources.title: Grant repository access to users and groups | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: If repository access level is limited to read-only, write operations like push or settings changes are rejected. Bitbucket Web UI path: `Settings → User and group access` to change roles. Change the user's role to "Write" or "Admin". With Read, only pull is possible; granting write permission enables push. Workspace administrators can access the User and group access section from the Settings page of the target repository and view/change user roles (Read, Write, Admin). Granting an Admin role to a repository is only performed by a Workspace administrator. Bitbucket has different role settings at the Workspace level and Repository level.

sources.title: Managing User Permissions for Bitbucket Repositories Access Granting - Medium
sources.claim: If repository access level is limited to read-only, write operations like push or settings changes are rejected. Bitbucket Web UI path: `Settings → User and group access` to change roles. With Read, only pull is possible; granting write permission enables push.

sources.title: Configure project permissions for users and groups | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: Workspace administrators can access the User and group access section from the Settings page of the target repository and view/change user roles (Read, Write, Admin). Bitbucket has different role settings at the Workspace level and Repository level.

sources.title: How do I gain access to Users and Groups? - Atlassian Community
sources.claim: Bitbucket Web UI path: `Settings → User and group access` to change roles. Change the user's role to "Write" or "Admin". Workspace administrators can access the User and group access section from the Settings page of the target repository and view/change user roles (Read, Write, Admin).

sources.title: Control access to private content in a workspace | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: If an organization has enabled IP whitelisting, access from outside the allowed network range will be rejected with a 403 error. Workspace administrator navigates to Settings → IP Whitelisting. Add an IP address range (e.g., `203.0.113.0/24`) to the whitelist.

sources.title: Access tokens for a repository | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: When using the REST API, the scope permissions of the Personal Access Token or App Password must also be checked. If the `repository:write` scope is not granted during token generation, push or commit creation via API will fail with a 403.

sources.title: Repository-level access token permissions | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: Specific operations such as repository deletion, protected branch settings changes, and deploy key management can only be performed by users with the Admin role. When using the REST API, the scope permissions of the Personal Access Token or App Password must also be checked. If the `repository:write` scope is not granted during token generation, push or commit creation via API will fail with a 403.

sources.title: API token permissions | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: Specific operations such as repository deletion, protected branch settings changes, and deploy key management can only be performed by users with the Admin role. When using the REST API, the scope permissions of the Personal Access Token or App Password must also be checked. If the `repository:write` scope is not granted during token generation, push or commit creation via API will fail with a 403.

sources.title: Bitbucket Cloud REST API scopes - Developer, Atlassian
sources.claim: Specific operations such as repository deletion, protected branch settings changes, and deploy key management can only be performed by users with the Admin role. When using the REST API, the scope permissions of the Personal Access Token or App Password must also be checked. If the `repository:write` scope is not granted during token generation, push or commit creation via API will fail with a 403.

sources.title: Bitbucket Git authentication: Use API token with scopes instead of password - YouTrack
sources.claim: When using the REST API, the scope permissions of the Personal Access Token or App Password must also be checked. If the `repository:write` scope is not granted during token generation, push or commit creation via API will fail with a 403. Check if Git's credential manager is caching old access tokens. Example commands for resetting cached credentials (Windows, macOS, Linux).

sources.title: View and configure the audit log | Bitbucket Data Center 10.3 | Atlassian Documentation
sources.claim: In Workspace Settings → Audit log, you can check if user permission change history is accurately recorded.

sources.title: Bitbucket Cloud audit log events - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: In Workspace Settings → Audit log, you can check if user permission change history is accurately recorded.

sources.claim: If an organization has enabled IP whitelisting, access from outside the allowed network range will be rejected with a 403 error. Workspace administrator navigates to Settings → IP Whitelisting. Add an IP address range (e.g., `203.0.113.0/24`) to the whitelist.

sources.claim: If an organization has enabled IP whitelisting, access from outside the allowed network range will be rejected with a 403 error.

sources.title: Solved: Trouble triggering Bitbucket REST api via JIRA aut... - Atlassian Community
sources.claim: If an organization has enabled IP whitelisting, access from outside the allowed network range will be rejected with a 403 error.

sources.title: Viewing Audited Events - Soteri Documentation
sources.claim: Call the Bitbucket API directly with curl to check for more detailed error messages.

sources.title: Controlling access to code | Bitbucket Data Center 10.3 - Atlassian Documentation
sources.claim: Bitbucket has different role settings at the Workspace level and Repository level. Even a Workspace administrator, if only granted a Read role for a specific repository, will be denied write operations on that repository with a 403 error.

unsupported_claims: Example Git error message: `remote: PERMISSION_DENIED: User <user@example.com> has insufficient permissions to access <workspace>/<repository>` (while the concept is strongly supported, the exact wording is not directly cited from official Bitbucket documentation).

Example REST API error message: `{ "type": "error", "error": { "message": "You do not have permission to access this resource", "detail": "User does not have write access to this repository" }, "status": 403 }` (while the concept is strongly supported, the exact JSON structure and detail field value are not directly cited from official Bitbucket documentation).
This is especially common for newly added team members or users whose permissions have been removed (observational, not directly documented as a cause by Bitbucket).
Check current IP address: `https://www.whatismyipaddress.com/` (an external tool, not an official Bitbucket instruction or factual claim about Bitbucket).

If an SSH key is registered as a repository-specific deploy key, its permission level (Read-only or Write) must also be considered. If a push from a CI/CD pipeline fails with a 403, check the deploy key's permissions (logical, but direct official Bitbucket documentation explicitly linking deploy key permissions to CI/CD push 403s is missing).

Open Bitbucket Web UI's Settings → User and group access page, clear browser cache, and try again. It may take a few minutes for permission changes to reflect (clearing browser cache is general troubleshooting; the exact 'few minutes' for permission reflection is plausible but not explicitly cited for Bitbucket).
improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.
Optional improvement: Provide direct official Bitbucket documentation links for the exact wording of Git and REST API error messages if they exist.
Optional improvement: Find direct Bitbucket documentation on deploy key permissions (Read-only/Write) and their explicit effect on CI/CD pushes.
Optional improvement: Consider rephrasing or adding a disclaimer for the use of external tools like `https://www.whatismyipaddress.com/`.
Optional improvement: Acknowledge that clearing browser cache and permission propagation times are general observations rather than strictly documented Bitbucket procedures.
Optional improvement: Add official or primary source URLs for unsupported claims.
